# Provider Principal Garage Corpus: Initial Grill Plan

## Source Inputs

- `docs/project_status/devnotes.md`, item 9
- `docs/planning/archive/project_status_retired_2026_06_08/features/parser_and_document_extraction/`
- `docs/planning/archive/project_status_retired_2026_06_08/features/inspection_location_readiness/`
- `docs/planning/inspection_address_helper/ce_principals_and_garages_normalized.md`

## Current Understanding

The dev notes request a database/list of principals and garages for AI context and inspection address support. Current provider/principal data is repository/reference-file based, not a governed Management-editable backend corpus.

The provider/principal/garage corpus is also needed to draft an inspection-address policy matrix and provider automation settings. Provider assignment to `always_image_based`, `prefer_address`, and `required_address` should be drafted from the corpus and `cedocumentmapper_v2.0` provider data, then reviewed by the user. Unknown providers should default to `prefer_address` for inspection-address policy and `Review auto` for Provider Automation Mode.

The corpus must be editable through governed Management `Provider Rules`; it is not status-only for the next usable demo. Editing includes Work Provider display names, known e-mail domains, the Principal Code field on each Work Provider, Provider Automation Mode, parser/provider rule activation status, inspection-address policy, known inspection-address lookup entries with evidence/source notes, work-provider-specific EVA readiness requirements where already represented by deterministic rules, and import/export of the provider/principal corpus.

Safety rules: referenced Work Providers cannot be deleted and may only be deactivated or archived; e-mail domains and Principal Codes must be unique enough to prevent ambiguous provider matching and unsafe Case/PO generation; changing inspection-address policy or Provider Automation Mode to a less restrictive value requires a reason and impact count for affected open cases; `required_address` override behavior remains Management-only and audited; corpus address edits require source/evidence notes; imports require preview diff before apply; parser/provider rule activation changes require dry-run impact checks against recent/open cases before save; affected provider automation readiness becomes `Configured, not verified` until rechecked; rollback restores the previous provider-rule version.

Optional case overview fields may be imported/stored when present but must not drive workflow, readiness, matching, or automation unless later first-class source material says otherwise. Overview-only fields include `insuredName`, `claimantName`, `thirdPartyName`, `claimNumber`, `policyReference`, `incidentDate`, `claimType`, `insurerName` only when source material explicitly provides it, and `repairerName` only as a source label for the Inspection Address/contact record.

Core workflow fields remain VRM, Work Provider, Principal Code, source/reference identifiers, instruction/evidence files, images/video, mileage, Inspection Address or `Image Based Assessment`, EVA readiness fields, and Date Due/Inspection Date.

Current Case/PO handling: when a user submits to EVA, CCC should allow the user to enter the Case/PO. The Case/PO pattern is uniform across providers: Principal Code + two-digit year + provider case sequence number. Future automated sequence discovery from Box is deferred. The planned future Box approach is to check folders under the Principal Code/name, find the highest current number, and add one. Longer-term automatic Case/PO generation by CCC after EVA retirement is also deferred.

Inspection Address can be any physical location where the vehicle is located, not only a repairer/garage/bodyshop. Garage, repairer, and bodyshop are labels or aliases for some inspection-address/contact records.

`Figures` / figures expected status is source-backed by the job-sheet research as a Garages sheet field, but it is not a core readiness or matching driver. If stored, it should be an optional contact/inspection-address attribute.

Corpus record types are `Work Provider` and `Inspection Address`.

Work Provider fields: display name, Principal Code, known e-mail domains, Provider Automation Mode, active/archived status, default source/channel notes where known, parser/provider rule set reference, inspection-address policy, deterministic EVA readiness overrides where supported, optional Box folder/code mapping for future sequence lookup, chaser/contact guidance where source-backed, and audit history.

Inspection Address fields: display name or location label, source labels such as garage/repairer/bodyshop/home/business, six-line EVA address, postcode, optional phone, optional email, optional figures expected status where source-backed, source/evidence note for address/contact details, provenance link where obtained from web/AI/source lookup, linked Work Providers where known, and active/archived status.

Corpus governance is single-Management approval/change. Because CE is a small business of roughly 10 staff, a second-review workflow would make normal correction too difficult. Management can edit all Provider Rules/corpus records directly, subject to validation, impact warnings, change reason for risky changes, and audit. Case Intake/Admin Staff may submit inspection addresses, phone/email corrections, and source notes from case detail; these enter a Management review queue and do not become active automatically. Engineers can view but not edit Provider Rules/corpus records unless a future workflow requires it.

No corpus change needs mandatory second review before becoming active. Risky Management edits instead require stronger inline confirmation, impact counts where applicable, and rollback.

Provider matching should not rely on aliases. A Work Provider has a name, Principal Code, and known e-mail domains. Provider/domain matching should use the sender e-mail domain after `@` where available, for example `john@carcompany.co.uk` matching Work Provider `CarCompany` with Principal Code `CCPY`. Provider configuration should be maintained so active provider matching is not ambiguous.

Provider Automation Mode controls whether populated data requires human review. `No auto` means staff do everything, including data input. `Review auto` means extraction, corpus, AI assistance, and other assistance tools may populate data, but staff review it before EVA. `AI Auto` means the case may proceed to EVA after a strict AI review passes. `Full auto` means the case may proceed straight to EVA with zero human interaction when every gate passes.

Control checks use a two-tier model: first check the global kill switch, then check the Work Provider-specific setting. Main operational settings for automation, AI, EVA submission, external enrichment, and outbound communications are per Work Provider.

Per-Work Provider settings under the global kill switches are Provider Automation Mode, AI assistance allowed, EVA submission allowed, External enrichment allowed, Outbound communications allowed, and Inspection Address policy. `AI assistance allowed` controls whether the provider may use AI when global AI is enabled. `EVA submission allowed` controls whether CCC may submit the provider's cases to EVA when global EVA submission is enabled. `External enrichment allowed` controls whether DVLA/DVSA, Document AI, Cloud Vision, web search, or similar live enrichment may run for the provider when global enrichment is enabled. `Outbound communications allowed` controls whether automated or assisted outbound e-mail/WhatsApp actions may run for the provider when global outbound is enabled.

Each populated case field that affects EVA readiness carries field-level provenance metadata. Field provenance records include `fieldName`, `value`, `sourceType`, `sourceLabel`, `sourceReference`, optional `confidence`, `reviewState`, optional `reviewedBy`, optional `reviewedAt`, and optional `notes`/`reason`. `sourceType` values include `staff`, `pdf_extraction`, `email_text`, `corpus`, `ai`, `dvla_dvsa`, `document_ai`, `cloud_vision`, `web_lookup`, `whatsapp`, and `manual_upload`. Field review states are `not_required`, `needs_review`, `reviewed`, and `conflict`. Case UI should show compact markers beside fields, such as `PDF`, `Corpus`, `AI`, `Web`, and `Staff`. Pipeline runs store a summary of fields populated or changed, with provenance and review state.

`Review auto` uses a case-level review action with field-level visibility. Case review shows all EVA-relevant fields with provenance markers and highlights fields in `needs_review` or `conflict`. Staff can edit any incorrect field, then click `Mark case reviewed for EVA`. The server records the field values/provenance states that existed at the time of review. The server sets remaining `needs_review` fields to `reviewed` only if no `conflict` fields remain. Conflicts must be resolved by editing the field, selecting the source/corpus value, or marking an allowed override reason. Review creates an audit event with actor, timestamp, field summary, changed fields, unresolved warnings if any, and readiness result. Staff corrections during review are logged as improvement signals so CCC can identify parser, corpus, provider-rule, and enrichment gaps over time.

Staff corrections must not automatically change active rules. Staff correction signals go to an improvement queue with case ID, Work Provider, field name, original value, corrected value, original provenance, correction actor/time, whether the correction affected EVA readiness, source file/message reference, and optional staff reason. Correction signals are classified as `parser_rule_candidate`, `corpus_update_candidate`, `provider_policy_candidate`, `enrichment_issue`, or `one_off_case_issue`. Management can review correction signals and manually apply Provider Rules/corpus changes. Audit logs must support filtering correction/improvement signals and exporting them to a file for developer analysis.

Management correction/improvement signals should be reviewed in an `Improvement Review` page or a tab inside `Provider Rules`. Improvement Review supports filters by Work Provider, field name, signal type, source type, date range, staff member, and affected EVA readiness. It supports grouped views by repeated correction pattern, case drill-through, original value versus corrected value, provenance/source reference, count of similar corrections, CSV export, and JSON export. Actions are `Mark one-off`, `Create corpus update`, `Create parser rule task`, `Create provider policy task`, `Ignore`, and `Export filtered`. Every Management disposition creates an audit event.

Stale or conflicting Work Provider and Inspection Address records use versioned edits and archive/replace rather than destructive overwrites.

Work Provider correction rules: do not delete a Work Provider if referenced by any case; allow Management to edit name, domains, Principal Code, and settings; if Principal Code changes, preserve the previous code in history because old Case/PO values may still reference it; if the provider is no longer used, mark it archived/inactive; if two provider records are duplicates, merge into one active record and keep the old ID as an internal redirect for history.

Inspection Address correction rules: do not delete an Inspection Address if referenced by any case; allow Management to edit address/contact details; if the address moved or is stale, archive the old record and create/update the active record; if two records are duplicates, merge them and keep old IDs as redirects for history; if a case source conflicts with corpus, fill the case from source first and create an Improvement Review item; if Management confirms the corpus is wrong, update/archive the corpus record; if source is wrong or one-off, mark the improvement item as one-off/ignored.

Every correction requires a reason for risky fields, writes audit before/after values, preserves case history, and marks affected provider automation readiness as `Configured, not verified` until rechecked.

Autofilled/enriched fields must carry visible markers showing what populated them, such as PDF extraction, AI-filled, location data, corpus data, DVLA/DVSA, or staff entry. Web/AI-derived addresses appear as flagged addresses with a provenance link showing where the address was obtained.

If source material and corpus conflict, source material fills the case first. CCC flags the conflict on the address and offers an option to submit the issue to Management for corpus review.

## Resolved Question Status

Provider corpus questions are resolved for now. Management owns Provider Rules browser edits; developer seeding is an initial setup path only.

## Recommended Starting Position

Build a governed editable corpus for deterministic matching, Principal Code/Case/PO support, inspection-address policy, and address assistance. Treat corpus edits as high-impact settings changes with validation, audit, import/export diffing, and rollback. Use `Inspection Address` as the canonical term aligned to EVA; garage, repairer, bodyshop, and inspection site are source labels or lookup aliases, not separate core entities. Drop `Insurer` from the core corpus model unless future first-class source material makes it workflow-relevant.
